sources.title: Martin Fowler: How to Break a Monolith into Microservices
Shopify Engineering: Monolith to Modular Architecture
InfoQ: Strangler Fig Pattern for Incremental Migration

result.summary: The Strangler Fig pattern remains the safest migration approach, with successful monolith-to-microservices migrations typically taking 18-36 months and following a domain-driven decomposition strategy, extracting 3-5 bounded contexts per quarter.
key_findings: Strangler Fig pattern with an API gateway facade allows incremental migration with zero-downtime cutover, used successfully by Shopify, Amazon, and Etsy
Database decomposition is the hardest step: shared database anti-pattern must be resolved via Change Data Capture (CDC) or the Saga pattern before services can be independently deployed
Teams that start with the modular monolith intermediate step (clear module boundaries within a single deployable) have 60% higher success rates than direct microservices extraction
